tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: From Heartbreak to Glory: Linda Noskova Wins Wimbledon, Honors Her Mother
stories.summary: Twenty-one-year-old Linda Noskova survived five missed championship points to win her first Grand Slam, then tearfully thanked her late mother from the court.

stories.headline: One Boy's Wave Changed Everything: How a 4-Year-Old Brought His Neighborhood Together
stories.summary: Four-year-old Roman Butzlaff's daily waves to neighbors transformed isolated strangers into genuine friends who now attend his soccer games, swimming lessons, and birthday parties.

stories.headline: From Crutches to World Cup History: Mikel Merino's Impossible Comeback
stories.summary: Mikel Merino became the first player ever to score winning World Cup knockout goals as a substitute in two different tournaments, both times entering after the 75th minute.

stories.headline: Grandmother's Dream Saved: One Viral Post Fills Her Salvadoran Restaurant With Love
stories.summary: Sophia's heartfelt Instagram plea to save her struggling grandmother's Maryland restaurant went viral, bringing a wave of loving customers in just days.

stories.headline: White Storks Return Home After 600 Years: Derbyshire Welcomes a Lost Species Back
stories.summary: Derbyshire welcomes white storks nesting for the first time since the 14th century, with a £300,000 predator-proof enclosure at Willington Wetlands ready to introduce the birds this summer.

stories.headline: Strangers in the Mud, Friends in Spirit: How One Vendor's Kindness Built Community
stories.summary: Twenty-seven-year-old entrepreneur Kai Emilio rallied strangers at a Singapore night market to free an elderly driver's van from the mud, then treated everyone to cold drinks.

stories.headline: From 12 Down to Glory: Wicklow's Stunning Comeback Claims Tailteann Cup
stories.summary: Wicklow rallied from 12 points down at halftime with a stunning 15-point turnaround to claim the Tailteann Cup and earn their place in the 2027 All-Ireland.

stories.headline: Against All Odds: Merab Dvalishvili Rallies From 8-0 Down to Beat Olympic Champion
stories.summary: Merab Dvalishvili defied early adversity to rally from an 8-0 deficit and beat Olympic wrestling gold medalist Henry Cejudo 11-8 in a thrilling hometown match.

stories.headline: A Town's Big Heart: Beaver Rallies to Rebuild After Tragic Cottonwood Fire
stories.summary: The town of Beaver rallied around firefighters battling the Cottonwood Fire across 97,000 acres, offering free lodging, early breakfasts, and goodie bags at local hotels.

stories.headline: Robot Surgeons Make History: Humanoid Robots Perform First-Ever Live Surgery
stories.summary: Surgeons at UC San Diego remotely guided humanoid robots through a live gallbladder removal, making history with the first-ever humanoid-performed surgery.

stories.headline: Largest Boreal Toad Rescue Ever: 3,500 Tadpoles Find New Home in Colorado
stories.summary: Over 3,500 endangered boreal toad tadpoles were released into historic Colorado breeding sites, marking the largest translocation yet in a multi-year recovery effort.

stories.headline: Amazon Rebounds: Deforestation Hits Decade Low With 38 Percent Drop
stories.summary: Brazil's Amazon rainforest records its lowest deforestation in a decade, marking a 38 percent drop under President Lula's push toward zero illegal deforestation by 2030.
